name: Christmas Eve Salad (Ensalada De Noche Buena)
recipeIngredient: Fresh pineapple or 1 20-oz can pineapple chunks
3 medium beets, cooked, peeled, and sliced
1 stick sugar cane, peeled and chopped (optional)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove the crown of fresh pineapple.
2. Peel the pineapple and remove any eyes; quarter it and remove the core.
3. Cut the pineapple into chunks. Alternatively, drain canned pineapple.
4. Peel the oranges and section them over a bowl to catch juice.
5. Peel and slice the bananas.
6. Core and slice the apple. Toss the apple slices with banana slices and orange sections along with their juices.
7. Drain all fruits.
8. Arrange the fruit pieces on a large lettuce-lined platter, including pineapple chunks, sliced beets, jicama, and sugar cane (if using).
9. Sprinkle peanuts and pomegranate seeds over the top.
10. Thinly mix mayonnaise or salad dressing with a small amount of milk to achieve a drizzling consistency.
11. Serve alongside the salad.
Makes 6 to 8 servings.
Comments: Marge omitted beets and added kiwi, which was served well with Miss Daisy's poppy seed dressing.
Source: Better Homes and Gardens Mexican Cookbook (1977)
